home.title: Seeing Single-Cell
home.subtitle: An interactive visual exploration of the mathematics behind
home.subtitleEnd: Play with the math. See what happens.
home.interactDesc: Hover, click, and drag on visualizations. Adjust sliders and parameters in real-time.
home.readDesc: Each section explains a concept, then shows it visually. Watch the math come alive.
home.experimentDesc: Change parameters and see the effects immediately. Build intuition through play.
home.ch4Desc: From thousands of dimensions to a 2D map. Understand PCA, t-SNE, and UMAP through interactive visualizations.

ch1.title: The Gene Expression Matrix
ch1.subtitle: Every single-cell experiment begins with a matrix — rows are cells, columns are genes, and each value tells us how much a gene is expressed.
ch1.step1Title: What Does the Data Look Like?
ch1.step2Title: Why Are There So Many Zeros?
ch1.bioZeroDesc: The gene is truly not expressed in that cell.
ch1.techDropoutDesc: The gene was expressed, but at such low levels that sequencing failed to capture it.
ch1.conceptText1: In scRNA-seq, we measure the expression of
ch1.conceptText5: The result is a matrix where each cell (row) has a “barcode” — its unique pattern of gene expression.
ch1.sparseDesc1: Look at the matrix above — notice the many white (zero) cells? This is called
ch1.sparseDesc2: and it’s a hallmark of single-cell data.
ch1.sparseDesc4: of all values are zero. This happens for two main reasons:
ch1.bioZeroFullDesc: The gene is truly not expressed in that cell. Cell-type specific genes will show zeros in cells where they are inactive.
ch1.techDropoutFullDesc: The gene was expressed, but at such low levels that the sequencing process failed to capture it. This is a key challenge in scRNA-seq analysis.
ch1.mathDesc4: is the number of cells and
ch1.mathDesc5: is the number of genes. The sparsity is defined as:
ch1.readRowsDesc: is a single cell. Cells of the same type (e.g., CD4 T cells) tend to have similar expression patterns — they’ll cluster together visually.
ch1.readColsDesc1: is a gene. Marker genes like
ch1.readColsDesc2: are highly expressed only in specific cell types, creating bright vertical “stripes” in the matrix.
ch1.tryItDesc: Hover over the matrix to see individual values. Click on a row or column to highlight it. Use the color scale slider to adjust contrast.
ch1.look1: Rows with similar patterns → same cell type
ch1.look2: Columns with many zeros → lowly expressed or cell-type specific
ch1.look3: Bright “hotspots” → marker genes in specific cell types
ch1.libSizeDefDesc: The total sum of UMI counts across all genes for each cell. This is the baseline measurement of sequencing depth.
ch1.exprFeaturesDefDesc: The number of genes with non-zero counts in each cell. Distinct from the total count — a cell may have high total counts but few unique genes.
ch1.sparsityExplain: Where Xᵢⱼ is the expression of gene j in cell i, n is number of cells, p is number of genes, and 𝟙[·] is the indicator function (1 if condition is true, 0 otherwise).

ch2.title: Data Distribution
ch2.subtitle: Not all genes are created equal. Explore how expression values are distributed across cells.
ch2.concept1: The distribution of a gene's expression values across all cells reveals important patterns. Housekeeping genes tend to be normally distributed, while cell-type markers show bimodal distributions (on or off).
ch2.try1: Select different genes from the dropdown — compare CD3D (T cell marker) vs B2M (housekeeping)
ch2.try2: Adjust the bin count to see how granularity affects the histogram's shape
ch2.try3: Toggle the KDE curve on/off to see how kernel smoothing works
ch2.try4: Watch how the statistics panel changes with each gene
ch2.histDesc: A histogram divides the data range into
ch2.histDescEnd: and counts how many values fall into each bin. It's simple and intuitive, but the shape depends on bin width and starting position.
ch2.kdeDesc: KDE places a smooth kernel (usually Gaussian) at each data point, then sums them up. The result is a continuous, smooth estimate of the probability density function.
ch2.bandwidthDesc2: in KDE controls the smoothness: too small → noisy and spiky, too large → over-smoothed. In the visualization above, try different genes to see how the optimal bandwidth changes with the data shape.
ch2.normalPatDesc: Housekeeping genes (e.g., GAPDH, B2M) are expressed in all cells. Their distribution is roughly symmetric around the mean.
ch2.bimodalPatDesc: Cell-type markers (e.g., CD3D) show two peaks: one at zero (cells that don't express it) and one at a higher value (cells that do).
ch2.zeroInfPatDesc: Lowly expressed genes have a huge spike at zero with a long right tail. This is the "dropout" problem in scRNA-seq.
ch2.kdeFormulaDesc: where f̂(x) is the estimated density, n is the number of cells, h is the bandwidth (smoothing parameter), K is the Gaussian kernel function, and x₁...xₙ are the observed expression values.

ch3.title: Preprocessing Trilogy
ch3.subtitle: Before clustering and visualization, scRNA-seq data goes through three essential preprocessing steps:
ch3.subtitleEnd: These steps correct technical noise and prepare the data for downstream analysis.
ch3.whyNormDesc: Different cells capture different amounts of mRNA during sequencing — this is a
ch3.whyNormDesc2: not biological. Cell A might have 10× more total counts than Cell B simply because it was more efficiently captured.
ch3.formulaDesc: 1) Divide by library size, × 10k  2) log1p to stabilize variance
ch3.interactDesc: Hover over the matrix to see cell details
ch3.tryNorm1: Toggle Library Size — cells become comparable
ch3.tryNorm2: Toggle × 10,000 — values scale to interpretable range
ch3.tryNorm3: Try log1p / log2 / ln — see how log compresses dynamic range
ch3.whyHVGDesc: Not all genes are informative. Housekeeping genes (like GAPDH) are expressed at similar levels across all cells. HVGs show high cell-to-cell variance and drive biological heterogeneity.
ch3.hvgInput: Data input: normalized expression matrix. HVGs identified by mean-variance relationship.
ch3.hvgHeatmapTitle: HVG Expression Across Cells (sorted by type)
ch3.hvgHeatmapDesc: Cells sorted by type. HVGs show heterogeneous expression across cells; uniform genes look the same everywhere.
ch3.poissonDesc: Points above dashed orange line have overdispersion
ch3.nbDesc: Points above purple line are most variable
ch3.whyScaleDesc: After normalization, genes still have different means. Scaling z-scores each gene to mean=0, std=1, giving equal weight in PCA.
ch3.scaleInput: Input: 20 HVGs from log-normalized data. We use a fixed set of top HVGs by variance for this demo.
ch3.subtitleFull: Before clustering and visualization, scRNA-seq data goes through three essential preprocessing steps:
ch3.step1Desc: Different cells capture different amounts of mRNA — this is a technical artifact, not biological. Cell A might have 10× more total counts than Cell B simply because it was more efficiently captured.
ch3.step2Desc: Not all genes are informative. Housekeeping genes (like GAPDH) are expressed at similar levels across all cells. HVGs show high cell-to-cell variance and drive biological heterogeneity.
ch3.hvgDataInput: Data input: normalized expression matrix. HVGs identified by mean-variance relationship.
ch3.poissonDesc2: Points above dashed orange line have overdispersion
ch3.nbDesc2: Points above purple line are most variable
ch3.step3Desc: After normalization, genes still have different means. Scaling z-scores each gene to mean=0, std=1, giving equal weight in PCA.
ch3.scaleFormulaDesc: Input: 20 HVGs from normalized matrix. Output: centered and scaled.
ch3.hvgExprTitle: HVG Expression Across Cells (sorted by type)
ch3.whyLogDesc1: Differences in log-values represent log-fold changes in expression. This is crucial for downstream analyses (PCA, clustering) based on Euclidean distances.
ch3.whyLogConclusion: Log-transformation promotes contributions from genes with strong relative differences, making biologically meaningful changes stand out.
ch3.compBiasDesc: When gene X is upregulated in cell A, sequencing resources are diverted to X, reducing coverage of all other non-DE genes. The net effect: non-DE genes in A appear downregulated compared to B.
ch3.compBiasWhy: This is why library size normalization alone is insufficient — it cannot distinguish between true downregulation and composition bias.
ch3.logFCDesc: log₂(x₂/x₁) = log₂(x₂) - log₂(x₁). The log-transformation converts multiplicative relationships into additive ones, which is what distance-based methods expect.
ch3.sizeFactorDesc: Each cell's size factor = its library size ÷ mean library size. After normalization, all cells have the same effective library size, making them directly comparable.
ch3.varDecompFormula: Total Variance = Technical Component + Biological Component
ch3.varDecompDesc: The trend line in the mean-variance plot estimates the technical component (sampling noise + technical variation). The biological component = total variance − technical component. Genes with the largest biological component are the most informative HVGs.
ch3.negBio: Negative biological components are normal — approximately half of genes will lie below the trend line.
ch3.equalWeightDesc: Without scaling, highly-expressed genes (like ribosomal genes) dominate PCA and clustering simply because of their magnitude, not because they carry the most biological information. Scaling ensures each gene contributes proportionally to its variance, not its expression level.
ch3.regressOutDesc: ScaleData can also regress out unwanted sources of variation, such as mitochondrial contamination (percent.mt) or cell cycle effects, further cleaning the data for downstream analysis.

home.title: 看见单细胞
home.subtitle: 一场关于
home.subtitleEnd: 背后数学原理的交互式视觉探索。动手操作数学公式，观察会发生什么。
home.interactDesc: 悬停、点击和拖拽可视化图表，实时调整滑块和参数。
home.readDesc: 每个小节先解释概念，再用可视化呈现，让数学公式活起来。
home.experimentDesc: 改变参数，立即看到效果，在操作中建立直觉。
home.ch4Desc: 从数千维度到2D地图。通过交互式可视化理解PCA、t-SNE和UMAP。

ch1.title: 基因表达矩阵
ch1.subtitle: 每个单细胞实验都从一个矩阵开始——行是细胞，列是基因，每个值告诉我们基因的表达量。
ch1.bioZeroDesc: 该基因在该细胞中确实不表达。
ch1.techDropoutDesc: 该基因有表达，但表达量太低，测序过程未能捕获。
ch1.conceptText1: 在单细胞RNA测序中，我们测量
ch1.conceptText5: 。结果是一个矩阵，每个细胞（行）都有一个"条形码"——其独特的基因表达模式。我们的数据集包含
ch1.sparseDesc1: 观察上面的矩阵——注意到许多白色（零值）单元格了吗？这叫做
ch1.sparseDesc4: 的值为零。这主要有两个原因：
ch1.bioZeroFullDesc: 该基因在该细胞中确实不表达。细胞类型特异性基因会在不活跃的细胞中显示为零。
ch1.techDropoutFullDesc: 该基因有表达，但表达量太低，测序过程未能捕获。这是单细胞RNA测序分析的关键挑战。
ch1.mathDesc5: 是基因数。稀疏性定义为：
ch1.readRowsDesc: 是一个细胞。相同类型的细胞（如CD4 T细胞）往往有相似的表达模式——在视觉上会聚集在一起。
ch1.readColsDesc1: 是一个基因。标记基因如
ch1.readColsDesc2: 只在特定细胞类型中高表达，在矩阵中形成明亮的垂直“条纹”。
ch1.tryItDesc: 将鼠标悬停在矩阵上查看单个值。点击行或列进行高亮。使用颜色刻度滑块调整对比度。
ch1.look1: 行模式相似 → 相同细胞类型
ch1.look2: 零值较多的列 → 低表达或细胞类型特异性
ch1.look3: 明亮“热点” → 特定细胞类型的标记基因
ch1.libSizeDefDesc: 每个细胞所有基因UMI计数的总和。这是测序深度的基本度量。
ch1.exprFeaturesDefDesc: 每个细胞中非零计数的基因数量。与总计数不同——一个细胞可能总count很高但检测到的基因种类很少。
ch1.sparsityExplain: 其中 Xᵢⱼ 是第i个细胞第j个基因的表达值，n是细胞数，p是基因数，𝟙[·]是指示函数（条件为真时值为1，否则为0）。

ch2.title: 数据分布
ch2.subtitle: 并非所有基因都一样。探索表达值如何在细胞间分布，发现不同分布形状揭示的生物学信息。
ch2.step2Title: 直方图 vs 核密度估计
ch2.step3Title: 单细胞RNA测序中常见的分布模式
ch2.concept1: 基因表达值在所有细胞中的分布揭示了重要模式。管家基因趋向于正态分布，而细胞类型标记基因呈现双峰分布（开或关）。
ch2.try1: 从下拉菜单选择不同基因——比较CD3D（T细胞标记）vs B2M（管家基因）
ch2.try2: 调整分箱数量，观察粒度如何影响直方图形状
ch2.try3: 开关KDE曲线，了解核平滑的工作原理
ch2.try4: 观察统计面板如何随每个基因变化
ch2.histDesc: 直方图将数据范围划分为
ch2.histDescEnd: ，并计算每个箱体中有多少值。简单直观，但形状取决于箱宽和起始位置。
ch2.kdeDesc: KDE在每个数据点放置一个平滑核（通常是高斯核），然后将它们求和。结果是概率密度函数的连续平滑估计。
ch2.bandwidthDesc2: 控制平滑度：太小→噪声大且尖刺多，太大→过度平滑。在上面的可视化中，尝试不同基因看看最优带宽如何随数据变化。
ch2.normalPatDesc: 管家基因（如GAPDH、B2M）在所有细胞中表达。它们的分布大致关于均值对称。
ch2.bimodalPatDesc: 细胞类型标记基因（如CD3D）显示两个峰值：一个在零（不表达的细胞），一个在较高值（表达的细胞）。
ch2.zeroInfPatDesc: 低表达基因在零处有巨大尖峰，伴有长右尾。这是单细胞RNA测序中的「丢失」问题。
ch2.kdeFormulaDesc: 其中 f̂(x) 是估计的概率密度函数，n 是细胞数，h 是带宽（平滑参数），K 是高斯核函数，x₁...xₙ 是观测到的表达值。

ch3.title: 预处理三部曲
ch3.subtitle: 在聚类和可视化之前，单细胞RNA测序数据需要经过三个关键预处理步骤：
ch3.subtitleEnd: 这些步骤校正技术噪声，为下游分析做准备。
ch3.whyNormDesc: 不同细胞在测序过程中捕获的mRNA量不同——这是一种
ch3.whyNormDesc2: ，而非生物学差异。细胞A的总计数可能比细胞B多10倍，仅仅是因为它被捕获得更高效。
ch3.formulaDesc: 1) 除以文库大小，× 1万  2) log1p稳定方差
ch3.interactDesc: 将鼠标悬停在矩阵上查看细胞详情
ch3.tryNorm1: 切换"文库大小"——细胞变得可比较
ch3.tryNorm2: 切换"× 10,000"——数值缩放到可解读范围
ch3.tryNorm3: 尝试 log1p / log2 / ln——观察对数如何压缩动态范围
ch3.whyHVG: 为什么要筛选高变基因？
ch3.whyHVGDesc: 并非所有基因都携带信息。管家基因（如GAPDH）在所有细胞中表达水平相似。高变基因在细胞间表现出高方差，是生物学异质性的驱动因素。
ch3.hvgInput: 数据输入：归一化后的表达矩阵。通过均值-方差关系识别高变基因。
ch3.hvgHeatmapTitle: 高变基因在全部细胞中的表达（按类型排序）
ch3.hvgHeatmapDesc: 细胞按类型排序。高变基因在不同细胞中表现出异质性表达；均匀表达的基因在所有细胞中看起来相同。
ch3.poissonDesc: 虚线橙色上方的点具有过度离散性
ch3.nbModel: 负二项分布: Var = μ + αμ²
ch3.nbDesc: 紫色线上方的点变异最大
ch3.whyScale: 为什么要数据缩放/中心化？
ch3.whyScaleDesc: 归一化后，基因仍有不同的均值。标准化将每个基因z-score化为均值=0、标准差=1，在PCA中给予相等权重。
ch3.scaleInput: 输入数据：来自log归一化数据的20个高变基因（按方差排序前N）。本演示使用固定数量。
ch3.sumNormDesc: 文库大小校正 + 对数变换
ch3.sumScaleDesc: 均值=0, 标准差=1, 为PCA做准备
ch3.subtitleFull: 在聚类和可视化之前，单细胞RNA测序数据需要经过三个关键预处理步骤：
ch3.step1Desc: 不同细胞在测序过程中捕获的mRNA量不同——这是一种技术性假象，而非生物学差异。对数标准化通过除以文库大小、乘以缩放因子、再取对数变换，消除这种技术偏差，使细胞之间可比。
ch3.step2Desc: 并非所有基因都携带信息。管家基因（如GAPDH）在所有细胞中表达水平相似。高变基因在细胞间表现出高方差，是生物学异质性的驱动因素。
ch3.hvgDataInput: 数据输入：对数标准化后的表达矩阵。通过均值-方差关系识别高变基因。
ch3.poissonDesc2: 虚线橙色上方的点具有过度离散性
ch3.nbDesc2: 紫色线上方的点变异最大
ch3.step3Desc: 对数标准化后，不同基因仍有不同的均值和方差。z分数中心化将每个基因标准化为均值=0、标准差=1，使所有基因在PCA中具有相等权重。
ch3.scaleFormulaDesc: 输入：来自对数标准化矩阵的20个高变基因。输出：已中心化，均值=0，标准差=1。
ch3.hvgExprTitle: 高变基因在全部细胞中的表达（按类型排序）
ch3.whyLogDesc1: 对数差异值代表了表达量的对数倍数变化。这对于基于欧氏距离的下游分析（PCA、聚类）至关重要。
ch3.whyLogConclusion: 对数变换放大了相对变化大的基因的贡献，使有生物学意义的变化更加突出。
ch3.compBiasDesc: 当基因X在细胞A中上调时，测序资源被X占用，导致其他非差异表达基因的覆盖度降低。最终效果：A中的非DE基因看起来比B中下调了。
ch3.compBiasWhy: 这就是为什么单纯的文库大小归一化不够——它无法区分真正的下调和组成偏差。
ch3.logFCDesc: log₂(x₂/x₁) = log₂(x₂) - log₂(x₁)。对数变换将乘法关系转化为加法关系，这正是基于距离的方法所需要的。
ch3.sizeFactorDesc: 每个细胞的大小因子 = 其文库大小 ÷ 平均文库大小。归一化后，所有细胞具有相同的有效文库大小，可以直接比较。
ch3.varDecompFormula: 总方差 = 技术组分 + 生物学组分
ch3.varDecompDesc: 均值-方差图中的趋势线估计了技术组分（采样噪声 + 技术变异）。生物学组分 = 总方差 − 技术组分。生物学组分最大的基因是最有信息量的高变基因。
ch3.negBio: 负的生物学组分是正常的——大约一半的基因会落在趋势线下方。
ch3.equalWeightDesc: 如果不进行标准化，高表达基因（如核糖体基因）仅仅因为其量级就会主导PCA和聚类结果，而不是因为它们携带最多的生物学信息。标准化确保每个基因按其方差而非表达水平成比例地贡献。
ch3.regressOut: 回归去除不需要的变异
ch3.regressOutDesc: 标准化还可以回归掉不需要的变异来源，如线粒体污染（percent.mt）或细胞周期效应，进一步清洗数据以供下游分析。

zh.footerBuilt: 使用 Next.js + p5.js 构建
